subject: Geography
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze the hydrological cycle and its role in sustaining water resources
Evaluate the causes and regional variations of water scarcity
Assess the effectiveness of multipurpose river projects
Critically examine the environmental and social impacts of dams
Investigate traditional water harvesting systems and their sustainability
Study government initiatives and policies for water management
Develop solutions for sustainable water conservation

lesson_plan.title: Hydrological Cycle and Water Sustainability
topics_covered: Impact of climate and human activity on water availability
student_activities: Create a detailed hydrological cycle diagram
Analyze how human activities affect the water cycle

lesson_plan.title: Water Scarcity: Causes and Case Studies
topics_covered: Definition and types of water scarcity
student_activities: Analyze a case study of water scarcity in an Indian city
Identify key factors causing water shortages

lesson_plan.title: Sustainable Water Management
student_activities: Design a plan for water conservation in their locality

lesson_plan.title: Multipurpose River Projects: Functions and Importance
topics_covered: Major river valley projects in India
student_activities: Mark major dams on a map of India
Research a river project and list its uses

lesson_plan.title: Environmental and Social Impacts of Dams
student_activities: Prepare arguments supporting or opposing large dams
Create a table comparing benefits and drawbacks

lesson_plan.title: Rainwater Harvesting and Traditional Water Systems
topics_covered: Traditional methods such as tankas, johads, and kuls
student_activities: Design a rainwater harvesting system for a building

lesson_plan.title: Traditional Irrigation Systems and Chapter Synthesis
student_activities: Explain bamboo drip irrigation with diagrams
Participate in a comprehensive chapter quiz

teacher_tip: Encourage students to connect the chapter concepts with real-world water issues such as groundwater depletion, climate change, and urban water shortages to build critical thinking.
